contest.title: 2025-2026 ICPC, NERC, Northern Eurasia Finals (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Official NERC tutorial; used to confirm the entropy loss formula, trie prefix contribution, reversed suffix-array grouping, and DSU small-to-large maintenance.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: For every context length k, compute the optimal language-model training loss over the given token texts.
annotation.constraints: Computing each k independently is quadratic, so all context lengths must be processed with shared string data structures.
annotation.core_idea: For a fixed context, the optimal next-token distribution is empirical frequency, and its contribution is |N_ctx| log |N_ctx| minus sum count(next) log count(next). For short contexts that are prefixes, build a trie and add each node's contribution to all applicable k. For exact-length contexts, reverse all texts and build one string with separators; contexts become prefixes of suffixes. Sort suffixes with suffix array/LCP, process k from large to small, and merge adjacent suffix components when their LCP reaches k. Each DSU component stores token count and a map of next-token frequencies; merging maps small-to-large lets the global loss be updated efficiently.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) or O(n log^2 n), depending on map implementation.
tricks: reverse strings so contexts become suffix prefixes

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: Compute prefix-context contributions with a trie and exact-length context contributions by suffix-array components merged via DSU and small-to-large maps.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n log n) or O(n log^2 n)

tags.evidence: Exact-length contexts are grouped by sorting reversed-text suffixes and using LCP values.
tags.description: Use suffix array and LCP information as a main string-processing structure.
tags.created_reason: The existing suffix-structures tag is broad; suffix array is a common precise retrieval target.

tags.evidence: Suffix-array components are merged by LCP events, and each component's token-count map is merged small-to-large.
tags.description: Maintain component data by merging smaller maps/sets into larger ones to bound total movement.
tags.created_reason: This DSU merge pattern is a frequent technique distinct from plain DSU connectivity.

tags.evidence: Prefix contexts of all strings are handled by building a trie and accumulating node contributions.
tags.description: Prefix tree over strings or token sequences.
tags.created_reason: Trie is a standard data structure and should be directly searchable.

tags.evidence: The loss formula is an empirical cross-entropy expression using x log x terms for context and next-token counts.
tags.description: Use entropy or cross-entropy formulas, typically with count log count expressions.
tags.created_reason: This problem's math component is information-theoretic, not just generic math.

tags.evidence: The suffix array and LCP array drive the exact-context grouping.
